children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In order to be eligible to receive a grant under this subchapter, a State library administrative agency shall submit a State plan to the Director once every 5 years, as determined by the Director.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 2 Duration
children.text: The State plan shall cover a period of 5 fiscal years.

children.heading: Revisions
children.label: 3 Revisions
children.text: If a State library administrative agency makes a substantive revision to its State plan, then the State library administrative agency shall submit to the Director an amendment to the State plan containing such revision not later than April 1 of the fiscal year preceding the fiscal year for which the amendment will be effective.
establish goals, and specify priorities, for the State consistent with the purposes of this subchapter;

describe activities that are consistent with the goals and priorities established under paragraph (1), the purposes of this subchapter, and , that the State library administrative agency will carry out during such year using such grant;section 9141 of this title
describe the procedures that such agency will use to carry out the activities described in paragraph (2);

describe the methodology that such agency will use to evaluate the success of the activities established under paragraph (2) in achieving the goals and meeting the priorities described in paragraph (1);
describe the procedures that such agency will use to involve libraries and library users throughout the State in policy decisions regarding implementation of this subchapter;

children.label: A
children.text: programs and activities under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [ et seq.] (including programs and activities under subparts 2 and 3 of part B of title II [ et seq., 6661 et seq.], and parts A and B of title IV [ et seq., 7171 et seq.], of such Act);20 U.S.C. 630120 U.S.C. 664120 U.S.C. 7101

children.label: i
children.text: the State’s activities carried out under subsections (b)(4) and (e)(1) of ; andsection 9837 of title 42

children.label: ii
children.text: the activities described in the State’s strategic plan in accordance with ;section 9837b(a)(4)(B)(i) of title 42

children.label: i
children.text: the activities carried out by the State workforce development board under section 101 of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act [];29 U.S.C. 3111

children.label: ii
children.text: the State’s one-stop delivery system established under section 121(e) of such Act []; and29 U.S.C. 3151(e)

children.label: iii
children.text: the activities carried out by the State in support of adult education and literacy under title II of such Act [ et seq.]; and29 U.S.C. 3271

children.label: D
children.text: other Federal programs and activities that relate to library services, including economic, business, and community development, health information, critical thinking skills, digital literacy skills, financial literacy and other types of literacy skills;
provide assurances that the State will comply with subsection (f); and

provide assurances satisfactory to the Director that such agency will make such reports, in such form and containing such information, as the Director may reasonably require to carry out this subchapter and to determine the extent to which funds provided under this subchapter have been effective in carrying out the purposes of this subchapter.

children.heading: Evaluation and report
children.label: c Evaluation and report
children.text: Each State library administrative agency receiving a grant under this subchapter shall independently evaluate, and report to the Director regarding, the activities assisted under this subchapter, prior to the end of the 5-year plan.

children.heading: Information
children.label: d Information
children.text: Each library receiving assistance under this subchapter shall submit to the State library administrative agency such information as such agency may require to meet the requirements of subsection (c).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall approve any State plan under this subchapter that meets the requirements of this subchapter and provides satisfactory assurances that the provisions of such plan will be carried out.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 2 Public availability
children.text: Each State library administrative agency receiving a grant under this subchapter shall make the State plan available to the public, including through electronic means.

children.label: A
children.text: immediately notify the State library administrative agency of such determination and the reasons for such determination;

children.label: B
children.text: offer the State library administrative agency the opportunity to revise its State plan;

children.label: C
children.text: provide technical assistance in order to assist the State library administrative agency in meeting the requirements of this section; and

children.label: D
children.text: provide the State library administrative agency the opportunity for a hearing.

children.label: I
children.text: obscene;

children.label: II
children.text: child pornography; or

children.label: III
children.text: harmful to minors; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is enforcing the operation of such technology protection measure during any use of such computers by minors; and

children.label: I
children.text: obscene; or

children.label: II
children.text: child pornography; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is enforcing the operation of such technology protection measure during any use of such computers.

children.heading: Access to other materials
children.label: 2 Access to other materials
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to prohibit a library from limiting Internet access to or otherwise protecting against materials other than those referred to in subclauses (I), (II), and (III) of paragraph (1)(A)(i).

children.heading: Disabling during certain use
children.label: 3 Disabling during certain use
children.text: An administrator, supervisor, or other authority may disable a technology protection measure under paragraph (1) to enable access for bona fide research or other lawful purposes.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A library covered by paragraph (1) shall certify the compliance of such library with the requirements of paragraph (1) as part of the application process for the next program funding year under this subchapter following the effective date of this subsection, and for each subsequent program funding year thereafter.

children.heading: Libraries with Internet safety policies and technology protection measures in place
children.label: i Libraries with Internet safety policies and technology protection measures in place
children.text: A library covered by paragraph (1) that has in place an Internet safety policy meeting the requirements of paragraph (1) shall certify its compliance with paragraph (1) during each annual program application cycle under this subchapter.

children.heading: Libraries without Internet safety policies and technology protection measures in place
children.label: ii Libraries without Internet safety policies and technology protection measures in place
I
children.text: for the first program year after the effective date of this subsection in which the library applies for funds under this subchapter, shall certify that it is undertaking such actions, including any necessary procurement procedures, to put in place an Internet safety policy that meets such requirements; and

children.label: II
children.text: for the second program year after the effective date of this subsection in which the library applies for funds under this subchapter, shall certify that such library is in compliance with such requirements.

children.heading: Waivers
children.label: iii Waivers
children.text: Any library subject to a certification under clause (ii)(II) that cannot make the certification otherwise required by that clause may seek a waiver of that clause if State or local procurement rules or regulations or competitive bidding requirements prevent the making of the certification otherwise required by that clause. The library shall notify the Director of the Institute of Museum and Library Services of the applicability of that clause to the library. Such notice shall certify that the library will comply with the requirements in paragraph (1) before the start of the third program year after the effective date of this subsection for which the library is applying for funds under this subchapter.

children.heading: Use of General Education Provisions Act remedies
children.label: A Use of General Education Provisions Act remedies
i
children.text: withhold further payments to the recipient under this subchapter,

children.label: ii
children.text: issue a complaint to compel compliance of the recipient through a cease and desist order, or

children.label: iii
children.text: enter into a compliance agreement with a recipient to bring it into compliance with such requirements.

children.heading: Recovery of funds prohibited
children.label: B Recovery of funds prohibited
children.text: The actions authorized by subparagraph (A) are the exclusive remedies available with respect to the failure of a library to comply substantially with a provision of this subsection, and the Director shall not seek a recovery of funds from the recipient for such failure.

children.heading: Recommencement of payments
children.label: C Recommencement of payments
children.text: Whenever the Director determines (whether by certification or other appropriate evidence) that a recipient of funds who is subject to the withholding of payments under subparagraph (A)(i) has cured the failure providing the basis for the withholding of payments, the Director shall cease the withholding of payments to the recipient under that subparagraph.

children.heading: Separability
children.label: 6 Separability
children.text: If any provision of this subsection is held invalid, the remainder of this subsection shall not be affected thereby.

children.heading: Child pornography
children.label: A Child pornography
children.text: The term “child pornography” has the meaning given such term in .section 2256 of title 18

children.label: i
children.text: taken as a whole and with respect to minors, appeals to a prurient interest in nudity, sex, or excretion;

children.label: ii
children.text: depicts, describes, or represents, in a patently offensive way with respect to what is suitable for minors, an actual or simulated sexual act or sexual contact, actual or simulated normal or perverted sexual acts, or a lewd exhibition of the genitals; and

children.label: iii
children.text: taken as a whole, lacks serious literary, artistic, political, or scientific value as to minors.

children.heading: Minor
children.label: C Minor
children.text: The term “minor” means an individual who has not attained the age of 17.

children.heading: Obscene
children.label: D Obscene
children.text: The term “obscene” has the meaning applicable to such term in .section 1460 of title 18

children.heading: Sexual act; sexual contact
children.label: E Sexual act; sexual contact
children.text: The terms “sexual act” and “sexual contact” have the meanings given such terms in .section 2246 of title 18
